methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Cohesive Robotics is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by David Pietrocola that has raised $5M+. The valuation universe comprises one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers in the robotics sector, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

A stage-progression methodology is applicable to this analysis. Using total capital raised of $5M as a proxy for seed-stage value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $12M. The peer-median method could not be reliably applied due to insufficient narrow-filter comparables; while 11 same-sector peers exist in the robotics universe, none met the threshold of three or more datapoints when filtered by identical stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to one applicable method, supports a valuation range of $11M (low) to $14M (high), with a median of $12M. The narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodology rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of same-stage peer multiples for validation and the limited visibility into Cohesive Robotics' unit economics, technology differentiation, and burn rate relative to the broader robotics peer set. Additional revenue, growth, or comparable-transaction data would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
